---
title: "exnex_test"
format: html
editor: visual
---

### Data preparation

```{r}
### Prepare model inputs
set.seed(99999)
# Set up data
rr_k_ctrl <- c(0.15,0.15, 0.20, 0.20)        # control response rate for each basket
rr_k_trt <- c(0.30,0.30, 0.50, 0.50)         # treatment response rate for each basket

K<-length(rr_k_ctrl)        # number of baskets

N_k_ctrl <- rep(50, K)     # number of control participants per basket
N_k_trt <- rep(50, K)      # number of treatment participants per basket
N_k <- N_k_ctrl + N_k_trt   # number of participants per basket (both arms combined)
N <- sum(N_k)               # total sample size
k_vec <- rep(1:K, N_k)      # N x 1 vector of basket indicators (1 to K)

z_vec<-NULL;
y<-NULL;
for(i in 1:K){ # for each basket repeat 0-control 1-trt according to the specifc Ns
  z_vec<-c(z_vec,rep(0:1,c(N_k_ctrl[i],N_k_trt[i]))) # treatment/control indicator
  y<-c(y,
       c(rbinom(N_k_ctrl[i],1,rr_k_ctrl[i]), # bernoulli for control
         rbinom(N_k_trt[i],1,rr_k_trt[i]))) #           for trt
}

thedata<-data.frame(y,basketID=k_vec,Treatment=z_vec)

```

### EXNEX Stan

Centered parametirization

```{r}
EXNEX_stan_1 <- "data {
  int<lower = 1> K;                   // number of baskets
  int<lower = 1> N;                   // total number of participants
  array[N] int<lower = 1, upper = K> k_vec;   // basket index per subject
  array[N] int<lower = 0, upper = 1> z_vec;   // treatment indicator (1=active)
  array[N] int<lower = 0, upper = 1> y;       // binary response

  // Mixture weights: EX vs NEX (must sum to 1)
  simplex[2] pMix;                    // pMix[1]=Pr(EX), pMix[2]=Pr(NEX)

  
}

parameters {
  // Basket-specific parameters used in the likelihood
  vector[K] beta0;                    // intercepts (control log-odds)
  vector[K] beta1;                    // treatment effects (log-OR)

  // EX hierarchical hyperparameters
  real mu0;
  real<lower=0> sigma0;
  real mu1;
  real<lower=0> sigma1;
  
  // NEX priors 
  real nex_mu0;                       // NEX mean for control-arm log-odds (intercept)
  real<lower=0> nex_sigma0;           // NEX SD for control-arm log-odds
  real nex_mu1;                       // NEX mean for treatment effect (log-OR)
  real<lower=0> nex_sigma1;           // NEX SD for treatment effect
}

model {
  // EX hyperpriors
  mu0 ~ normal(0, 1);
  sigma0 ~ normal(0, 10);            // half-normal due to <lower=0>
  mu1 ~ normal(0, 1);
  sigma1 ~ normal(0, 10);            // half-normal due to <lower=0>
  
  // NEX hyperpriors
  nex_mu0 ~ normal(0, 1);
  nex_sigma0 ~ normal(0, 2.5);            // half-normal due to <lower=0>
  nex_mu1 ~ normal(0, 1);
  nex_sigma1 ~ normal(0, 2.5);            // half-normal due to <lower=0>

  // EX/NEX mixture prior for each basket's (beta0[k], beta1[k])

  for (k in 1:K) {
    real lp_ex =
      normal_lpdf(beta0[k] | mu0, sigma0) +
      normal_lpdf(beta1[k] | mu1, sigma1);

    real lp_nex =
      normal_lpdf(beta0[k] | nex_mu0, nex_sigma0) +
      normal_lpdf(beta1[k] | nex_mu1, nex_sigma1);

    target += log_mix(pMix[1], lp_ex, lp_nex);
  }

  // Likelihood
  for (i in 1:N) {
    y[i] ~ bernoulli_logit(beta0[k_vec[i]] + beta1[k_vec[i]] * z_vec[i]);
  }
}

generated quantities {
  // Posterior responsibilities Pr(EX|...) and Pr(NEX|...) per basket
  matrix[K, 2] post_prob;

  // A draw of the latent class label (like dcat) per basket
  array[K] int<lower=1, upper=2> class_k;   // 1=EX, 2=NEX

  // Optional: implied response probabilities under control/active per basket
  vector[K] p_control;
  vector[K] p_active;

  for (k in 1:K) {
    real lp_ex =
      log(pMix[1]) +
      normal_lpdf(beta0[k] | mu0, sigma0) +
      normal_lpdf(beta1[k] | mu1, sigma1);

    real lp_nex =
      log(pMix[2]) +
      normal_lpdf(beta0[k] | nex_mu0, nex_sigma0) +
      normal_lpdf(beta1[k] | nex_mu1, nex_sigma1);

    // convert to probabilities
    {
      vector[2] lp;
      vector[2] pr;
      lp[1] = lp_ex;
      lp[2] = lp_nex;
      pr = softmax(lp);

      post_prob[k, 1] = pr[1];
      post_prob[k, 2] = pr[2];
      class_k[k] = categorical_rng(pr);
    }

    p_control[k] = inv_logit(beta0[k]);
    p_active[k]  = inv_logit(beta0[k] + beta1[k]);
  }
}"
```

```{r rsetup, include=FALSE,eval=TRUE}
library(rstan)
library(bayesplot)
library(ggplot2)
library(kableExtra)
```

###Setup and run sampler

```{r}
# Create list with input values for Stan model
data_input_1 <- list(
  K = K,                # number of subgroups
  N = N,                # total sample size
  N_k = N_k,            # sample size per basket
  k_vec = k_vec,        # N x 1 vector of subgroup indicators
  z_vec = z_vec,        # N x 1 vector of active treatment arm indicators
  y = y,                 # N x 1 vector of binary responses
  pMix = c(0.5, 0.5)
  )



### Compile and fit model
options(mc.cores = parallel::detectCores())
# Compile model (only run the line below once for a simulation study - compilation not dependent on any
# simulation inputs as defined by scenarios or on simulated data)
stan_mod_1 <- stan_model(model_code = EXNEX_stan_1)
# Fit model
nsamps <- 30000       # number of posterior samples (after removing burn-in) per chain
nburnin <- 1000       # number of burn-in samples to remove at beginning of each chain
nchains <- 4          # number of chains
#BHM_pars_1 <- c("beta","mu0", "sigma0","mu1", "sigma1", "beta","beta_tr")     # parameters to sample
start_time <- Sys.time()
stan_fit_2 <- sampling(stan_mod_1, data = data_input_1, 
                       #pars = BHM_pars_1,
                       iter = nsamps + nburnin, warmup = nburnin, chains = nchains)

end_time <- Sys.time()
end_time - start_time
post_draws <- as.matrix(stan_fit_2)           # posterior samples of each parameter
```

```{r}
EXNEX_stan_2 <- "
data {
  int<lower = 1> K;                         // number of baskets
  int<lower = 1> N;                         // total number of participants
  array[N] int<lower = 1, upper = K> k_vec; // basket index per subject
  array[N] int<lower = 0, upper = 1> z_vec; // treatment indicator (1=active)
  array[N] int<lower = 0, upper = 1> y;     // binary response

  simplex[2] pMix;                          // pMix[1]=Pr(EX), pMix[2]=Pr(NEX)
}

parameters {
  // EX hyperparameters
  real mu0;
  real<lower=0> sigma0;
  real mu1;
  real<lower=0> sigma1;

  // NEX hyperparameters (you made these parameters, not fixed data)
  real nex_mu0;
  real<lower=0> nex_sigma0;
  real nex_mu1;
  real<lower=0> nex_sigma1;

  // Non-centered (raw) basket-level parameters for EX component
  vector[K] beta0_ex_raw;
  vector[K] beta1_ex_raw;

  // Non-centered (raw) basket-level parameters for NEX component
  vector[K] beta0_nex_raw;
  vector[K] beta1_nex_raw;
}

transformed parameters {
  // Component-specific basket-level coefficients
  vector[K] beta0_ex = mu0     + sigma0     * beta0_ex_raw;
  vector[K] beta1_ex = mu1     + sigma1     * beta1_ex_raw;

  vector[K] beta0_nex = nex_mu0 + nex_sigma0 * beta0_nex_raw;
  vector[K] beta1_nex = nex_mu1 + nex_sigma1 * beta1_nex_raw;
}

model {
  // EX hyperpriors
  mu0 ~ normal(0, 2.5);
  sigma0 ~ normal(0, 2.5);   // half-normal via <lower=0>
  mu1 ~ normal(0, 2.5);
  sigma1 ~ normal(0, 2.5);

  // NEX hyperpriors
  nex_mu0 ~ normal(0, 1);
  nex_sigma0 ~ normal(0, 1);
  nex_mu1 ~ normal(0, 1);
  nex_sigma1 ~ normal(0, 1);

  // Non-centered raw priors
  beta0_ex_raw  ~ normal(0, 1);
  beta1_ex_raw  ~ normal(0, 1);
  beta0_nex_raw ~ normal(0, 1);
  beta1_nex_raw ~ normal(0, 1);

  // Basket-level mixture likelihood (marginalized indicator)
  {
    vector[K] lp_ex  = rep_vector(0.0, K);
    vector[K] lp_nex = rep_vector(0.0, K);

    // accumulate log-likelihood contributions per basket under each component
    for (i in 1:N) {
      int k = k_vec[i];
      lp_ex[k]  += bernoulli_logit_lpmf(y[i] | beta0_ex[k]  + beta1_ex[k]  * z_vec[i]);
      lp_nex[k] += bernoulli_logit_lpmf(y[i] | beta0_nex[k] + beta1_nex[k] * z_vec[i]);
    }

    // mix at the basket level
    for (k in 1:K) {
      target += log_mix(pMix[1], lp_ex[k], lp_nex[k]);
    }
  }
}

generated quantities {
  matrix[K, 2] post_prob;
  array[K] int<lower=1, upper=2> class_k;

  vector[K] p_control;
  vector[K] p_active;

  // recompute per-basket responsibilities using the basket-level likelihood pieces
  {
    vector[K] lp_ex  = rep_vector(0.0, K);
    vector[K] lp_nex = rep_vector(0.0, K);

    for (i in 1:N) {
      int k = k_vec[i];
      lp_ex[k]  += bernoulli_logit_lpmf(y[i] | beta0_ex[k]  + beta1_ex[k]  * z_vec[i]);
      lp_nex[k] += bernoulli_logit_lpmf(y[i] | beta0_nex[k] + beta1_nex[k] * z_vec[i]);
    }

    for (k in 1:K) {
      vector[2] lp;
      vector[2] pr;

      lp[1] = log(pMix[1]) + lp_ex[k];
      lp[2] = log(pMix[2]) + lp_nex[k];
      pr = softmax(lp);

      post_prob[k, 1] = pr[1];
      post_prob[k, 2] = pr[2];
      class_k[k] = categorical_rng(pr);

      // mixture-averaged control/active response probabilities for basket k
      p_control[k] = pr[1] * inv_logit(beta0_ex[k]) +
                     pr[2] * inv_logit(beta0_nex[k]);

      p_active[k]  = pr[1] * inv_logit(beta0_ex[k]  + beta1_ex[k]) +
                     pr[2] * inv_logit(beta0_nex[k] + beta1_nex[k]);
    }
  }
}
"
```

```{r}
### Compile and fit model
options(mc.cores = parallel::detectCores())
# Compile model (only run the line below once for a simulation study - compilation not dependent on any
# simulation inputs as defined by scenarios or on simulated data)
stan_mod_1 <- stan_model(model_code = EXNEX_stan_2)
# Fit model
nsamps <- 30000       # number of posterior samples (after removing burn-in) per chain
nburnin <- 1000       # number of burn-in samples to remove at beginning of each chain
nchains <- 4          # number of chains
#BHM_pars_1 <- c("beta","mu0", "sigma0","mu1", "sigma1", "beta","beta_tr")     # parameters to sample
start_time <- Sys.time()
stan_fit_2 <- sampling(stan_mod_1, data = data_input_1, 
                       #pars = BHM_pars_1,
                       iter = nsamps + nburnin, warmup = nburnin, chains = nchains)

end_time <- Sys.time()
end_time - start_time
post_draws <- as.matrix(stan_fit_2)           # posterior samples of each parameter
```

### Non-centered 2

From Jules

```{r}

EXNEX_stan_3 <- "data {
  int<lower = 1> K;
  int<lower = 1> N;
  array[N] int<lower = 1, upper = K> k_vec;
  array[N] int<lower = 0, upper = 1> z_vec;
  array[N] int<lower = 0, upper = 1> y;

  simplex[2] pMix;  // pMix[1]=EX, pMix[2]=NEX
}

parameters {
  // EX hyperparameters
  real mu0;
  real<lower=0> sigma0;
  real mu1;
  real<lower=0> sigma1;

  // NEX hyperparameters (if you truly want to estimate them)
  real nex_mu0;
  real<lower=0> nex_sigma0;
  real nex_mu1;
  real<lower=0> nex_sigma1;

  // non-centered basket effects for EX and NEX
  vector[K] beta0_ex_raw;
  vector[K] beta1_ex_raw;
  vector[K] beta0_nex_raw;
  vector[K] beta1_nex_raw;
}

transformed parameters {
  vector[K] beta0_ex  = mu0      + sigma0      * beta0_ex_raw;
  vector[K] beta1_ex  = mu1      + sigma1      * beta1_ex_raw;
  vector[K] beta0_nex = nex_mu0  + nex_sigma0  * beta0_nex_raw;
  vector[K] beta1_nex = nex_mu1  + nex_sigma1  * beta1_nex_raw;
}

model {
  // hyperpriors (yours)
  mu0 ~ normal(0, 2.5);
  sigma0 ~ normal(0, 2.5);
  mu1 ~ normal(0, 2.5);
  sigma1 ~ normal(0, 2.5);

  nex_mu0 ~ normal(0, 1);
  nex_sigma0 ~ normal(0, 1);
  nex_mu1 ~ normal(0, 1);
  nex_sigma1 ~ normal(0, 1);

  // raw priors
  beta0_ex_raw  ~ normal(0, 1);
  beta1_ex_raw  ~ normal(0, 1);
  beta0_nex_raw ~ normal(0, 1);
  beta1_nex_raw ~ normal(0, 1);

  // basket-level mixture likelihood
  {
    vector[K] lp_ex  = rep_vector(0.0, K);
    vector[K] lp_nex = rep_vector(0.0, K);

    for (i in 1:N) {
      int k = k_vec[i];
      lp_ex[k]  += bernoulli_logit_lpmf(y[i] | beta0_ex[k]  + beta1_ex[k]  * z_vec[i]);
      lp_nex[k] += bernoulli_logit_lpmf(y[i] | beta0_nex[k] + beta1_nex[k] * z_vec[i]);
    }

    for (k in 1:K) {
      target += log_mix(pMix[1], lp_ex[k], lp_nex[k]);
    }
  }
}

generated quantities {
  matrix[K, 2] post_prob;
  array[K] int<lower=1, upper=2> class_k;

  vector[K] p_control;
  vector[K] p_active;

  {
    vector[K] lp_ex  = rep_vector(0.0, K);
    vector[K] lp_nex = rep_vector(0.0, K);

    for (i in 1:N) {
      int k = k_vec[i];
      lp_ex[k]  += bernoulli_logit_lpmf(y[i] | beta0_ex[k]  + beta1_ex[k]  * z_vec[i]);
      lp_nex[k] += bernoulli_logit_lpmf(y[i] | beta0_nex[k] + beta1_nex[k] * z_vec[i]);
    }

    for (k in 1:K) {
      vector[2] lp;
      vector[2] pr;

      lp[1] = log(pMix[1]) + lp_ex[k];
      lp[2] = log(pMix[2]) + lp_nex[k];
      pr = softmax(lp);

      post_prob[k,1] = pr[1];
      post_prob[k,2] = pr[2];
      class_k[k] = categorical_rng(pr);

      // mixture-averaged probabilities (optional)
      p_control[k] =
        pr[1] * inv_logit(beta0_ex[k]) +
        pr[2] * inv_logit(beta0_nex[k]);

      p_active[k] =
        pr[1] * inv_logit(beta0_ex[k] + beta1_ex[k]) +
        pr[2] * inv_logit(beta0_nex[k] + beta1_nex[k]);
    }
  }
}"

```

```{r}

### Compile and fit model
options(mc.cores = parallel::detectCores())
# Compile model (only run the line below once for a simulation study - compilation not dependent on any
# simulation inputs as defined by scenarios or on simulated data)
stan_mod_1 <- stan_model(model_code = EXNEX_stan_3)
# Fit model
nsamps <- 30000       # number of posterior samples (after removing burn-in) per chain
nburnin <- 1000       # number of burn-in samples to remove at beginning of each chain
nchains <- 4          # number of chains
#BHM_pars_1 <- c("beta","mu0", "sigma0","mu1", "sigma1", "beta","beta_tr")     # parameters to sample
start_time <- Sys.time()
stan_fit_2 <- sampling(stan_mod_1, data = data_input_1, 
                       #pars = BHM_pars_1,
                       iter = nsamps + nburnin, warmup = nburnin, chains = nchains)

end_time <- Sys.time()
end_time - start_time
post_draws <- as.matrix(stan_fit_2)           # posterior samples of each parameter
```

###

```{r}

test_model1 <- "
data {
  int<lower=1> K;
  int<lower=1> N;
  array[N] int<lower=1, upper=K> k_vec;
  array[N] int<lower=0, upper=1> z_vec;
  array[N] int<lower=0, upper=1> y;


  // fixed NEX priors, stratum-specific
  vector[K] nex_mu0;
  vector<lower=0>[K] nex_sigma0;
  vector[K] nex_mu1;
  vector<lower=0>[K] nex_sigma1;
}

parameters {
  vector<lower=0, upper=1>[K] pi; // Mixture prob
  // EX hyperparameters
  real mu0;
  real<lower=0> sigma0;
  real mu1;
  real<lower=0> sigma1;

  // non-centered stratum parameters for each component
  vector[K] beta0_ex_raw;
  vector[K] beta1_ex_raw;
  vector[K] beta0_nex_raw;
  vector[K] beta1_nex_raw;
}

transformed parameters {
  vector[K] beta0_ex  = mu0 + sigma0 * beta0_ex_raw;
  vector[K] beta1_ex  = mu1 + sigma1 * beta1_ex_raw;

  // NEX: no pooling; each stratum uses its fixed mean/sd
  vector[K] beta0_nex = nex_mu0 + nex_sigma0 .* beta0_nex_raw;
  vector[K] beta1_nex = nex_mu1 + nex_sigma1 .* beta1_nex_raw;
}

model {
  pi ~ beta(50, 50);
  // EX hyperpriors (example; tune as desired)
  mu0 ~ normal(0, 10);
  sigma0 ~ normal(0, 1);
  mu1 ~ normal(0, 10);
  sigma1 ~ normal(0, 1);

  // raw priors (non-centered)
  beta0_ex_raw  ~ normal(0, 1);
  beta1_ex_raw  ~ normal(0, 1);
  beta0_nex_raw ~ normal(0, 1);
  beta1_nex_raw ~ normal(0, 1);

  // stratum-level mixture likelihood
  {
    vector[K] ll_ex  = rep_vector(0.0, K);
    vector[K] ll_nex = rep_vector(0.0, K);

    for (i in 1:N) {
      int k = k_vec[i];
      ll_ex[k]  += bernoulli_logit_lpmf(y[i] | beta0_ex[k]  + beta1_ex[k]  * z_vec[i]);
      ll_nex[k] += bernoulli_logit_lpmf(y[i] | beta0_nex[k] + beta1_nex[k] * z_vec[i]);
    }

    for (k in 1:K)
      target += log_mix(pi[K], ll_ex[k], ll_nex[k]);
  }
}

generated quantities {
  // posterior responsibility per stratum + stratum arm probabilities
  matrix[K, 2] post_prob;
  vector[K] p_control;
  vector[K] p_treat;

  {
    vector[K] ll_ex  = rep_vector(0.0, K);
    vector[K] ll_nex = rep_vector(0.0, K);

    for (i in 1:N) {
      int k = k_vec[i];
      ll_ex[k]  += bernoulli_logit_lpmf(y[i] | beta0_ex[k]  + beta1_ex[k]  * z_vec[i]);
      ll_nex[k] += bernoulli_logit_lpmf(y[i] | beta0_nex[k] + beta1_nex[k] * z_vec[i]);
    }

    for (k in 1:K) {
      vector[2] lp = [ log(pMix[1]) + ll_ex[k],  log(pMix[2]) + ll_nex[k] ]';
      vector[2] pr = softmax(lp);

      post_prob[k,1] = pr[1];
      post_prob[k,2] = pr[2];

      // mixture-averaged arm probabilities
      p_control[k] =
        pr[1] * inv_logit(beta0_ex[k]) +
        pr[2] * inv_logit(beta0_nex[k]);

      p_treat[k] =
        pr[1] * inv_logit(beta0_ex[k] + beta1_ex[k]) +
        pr[2] * inv_logit(beta0_nex[k] + beta1_nex[k]);
    }
  }
}"

```


###Setup and run sampler

```{r}
# Create list with input values for Stan model
data_input_1 <- list(
  K = K,                # number of subgroups
  N = N,                # total sample size
  N_k = N_k,            # sample size per basket
  k_vec = k_vec,        # N x 1 vector of subgroup indicators
  z_vec = z_vec,        # N x 1 vector of active treatment arm indicators
  y = y,                 # N x 1 vector of binary responses
  nex_mu0 = c(0, 0, 0, 0),
  nex_sigma0 = c(5, 5, 5, 5),
  nex_mu1 = c(0, 0, 0, 0),
  nex_sigma1 = c(5, 5, 5, 5)
  )



### Compile and fit model
options(mc.cores = parallel::detectCores())
# Compile model (only run the line below once for a simulation study - compilation not dependent on any
# simulation inputs as defined by scenarios or on simulated data)
stan_mod_1 <- stan_model(model_code = test_model1)
# Fit model
nsamps <- 30000       # number of posterior samples (after removing burn-in) per chain
nburnin <- 1000       # number of burn-in samples to remove at beginning of each chain
nchains <- 4          # number of chains
#BHM_pars_1 <- c("beta","mu0", "sigma0","mu1", "sigma1", "beta","beta_tr")     # parameters to sample
start_time <- Sys.time()
stan_fit_2 <- sampling(stan_mod_1, data = data_input_1, 
                       #pars = BHM_pars_1,
                       iter = nsamps + nburnin, warmup = nburnin, chains = nchains)

end_time <- Sys.time()
end_time - start_time
post_draws <- as.matrix(stan_fit_2)           # posterior samples of each parameter
```
